title: M. Design in Interaction Design
content: The M.Des in Interaction Design trains designers to choreograph intuitive, engaging and meaningful exchanges between people and the digital–physical systems that now shape everyday life. Rooted in user-centred and research-driven practice, the programme blends behavioural insight with technological fluency so that graduates can imagine, prototype and validate future-ready products, services and experiences across screens, spaces and devices. What Students Will Learn Graduation in any discipline with minimum 50% marks. Students with a UCEED / CUET score will be given preference in the merit list. Graduates can step into roles such as: UX Designer / Interaction Designer / Product (Digital) Designer / Service Designer / Experience Strategist / Design Researcher / Accessibility & Inclusive-Design Specialist / AR-VR Experience Designer / Conversational UX Designer / IoT Interaction Designer / Data-Driven UX Analyst / Design Entrepreneur / Design Educator or Academic Researcher 01 Master diverse user-research tools (interviews, observations, surveys, analytics). 02 Understand people within their social and cultural contexts and translate findings into design strategies. 03 Cultivate critical thinking, data-driven storytelling and systems thinking. 04 Graduate “equipped to design with empathy, clarity and purpose.” 01 Design Knowledge – Apply theories of HCI, visual design and technology to solve complex interaction problems. 02 Problem Analysis – Frame user needs through rigorous qualitative & quantitative research. 03 Solution Development – Prototype and iterate interactive systems that balance usability, aesthetics, feasibility and sustainability. 04 Research & Evaluation – Plan experiments, collect data and use metrics to validate design decisions. 05 Modern Tool Usage – Use industry platforms (Figma, AR/VR toolkits, front-end frameworks, AI prototyping tools) effectively. 06 Design & Society – Assess cultural, social and environmental impacts of interactive technologies. 07 Ethics – Embed privacy, accessibility and ethical AI principles in design practice. 08 Teamwork – Function as contributor and leader in cross-functional teams. 09 Communication – Present concepts and research convincingly through visual, verbal and written media. 10 Project Management & Entrepreneurship – Plan, scope, budget and pitch interaction projects or start-ups. 11 Sustainability – Advocate for eco-efficient and durable digital experiences. 12 Lifelong Learning – Continuously adapt to emerging tools, paradigms and societal shifts. 01 Innovative Practice – Create impactful interaction solutions that blend creativity with evidence-based methods. 02 Societal Contribution – Address real-world challenges ethically, inclusively and sustainably. 03 Leadership & Collaboration – Excel in multidisciplinary teams, demonstrating communication and project-management skills. 04 Lifelong Learning & Enterprise – Pursue advanced research, entrepreneurial ventures or continuous up-skilling in the evolving design landscape. 01 Design intuitive, inclusive and emotionally engaging interactions for diverse user groups. 02 Translate deep user research into evidence-based prototypes and validated experience strategies. 03 Integrate AR/VR, IoT and AI responsibly to augment human experience. 04 Lead multidisciplinary initiatives that deliver human-centred digital transformation.
